mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 7.0 percent, it signals sufficient labor market slack absorption to sustain consumer spending growth, which drives corporate revenue expansion and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative or flat 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 7.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with accelerating nonfarm payrolls indicate synchronized consumer and business confidence, which amplifies aggregate demand and pushes S&P 500 returns higher over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising HOUST and PAYEMS fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production exceeding 100 on the 2017 base index confirms robust manufacturing output growth, which immediately boosts corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 returns within the following week.
failure_condition: Rank IC for INDPRO > 100 remains below 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window, or forward 5-day returns show no statistical difference from baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP directly expands the earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, producing a persistent upward drift in equity valuations over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth fails to correlate with positive 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of historical observations.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the CPIAUCSL index exceeding 0.3 percent triggers market repricing of higher discount rates and potential Federal Reserve tightening, which compresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL MoM exceeds 0.3 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm labor market tightening, which reduces wage-driven inflation fears and allows equities to re-rate higher within the following week.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS acceleration and UNRATE decline signal produces negative or flat 5-day returns in more than 55% of walk-forward periods.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter contraction in residential fixed investment exceeding 5 percent signals a housing sector downturn that drags down aggregate demand, reducing corporate earnings expectations and depressing S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of instances where RSAFS declines exceed 5 percent over a rolling quarter.
